orientation: Practise prime factorisation, hcf and lcm using the same structure from the learn lesson.

worked_example.question: Find HCF and LCM of 18 and 30

questions.question: Write 48 as a product of primes
questions.hint: Use a factor tree until all branches are prime.

questions.question: Find the HCF of 12 and 18
questions.hint: Use prime factors or factor lists.

questions.question: Find HCF and LCM of 45 and 60
Two numbers have HCF 6 and LCM 180. One number is 30. Find the other.
questions.hint: Use HCF × LCM = product of the two numbers.

common_mistakes.correction: HCF uses common prime factors at the lowest powers.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing a prime factor in LCM.
common_mistakes.correction: LCM must include all primes appearing in either number at highest powers.

common_mistakes.mistake: Stopping factor trees before reaching primes.
common_mistakes.correction: Continue factorising until every branch is prime.
